summary: Matched a broad batch of tiny 24-byte routines across game-mode, ground/stage, menu, and item code by replacing stubs with simple accessors, setters, flag writes, velocity resets, and repeated float-field adders. The PR also added or expanded static headers, structs, prototypes, and local data declarations needed for those matches. There were no PR comments or reviews in the slice, so intent beyond the diff is inferred from the code changes.
what_changed: Implemented simple game-mode accessors/setters: fn_8016F1F0 returns lbl_803D5A4C[idx].x0, fn_80179F6C writes lbl_8046E38C[idx], and gm_80188454 returns lbl_80473700[idx + 2].
Expanded gm_1601.static.h with typed static data needed by those routines, including struct lbl_803D5A4C_t, struct lbl_80473700_t, lbl_803D5A4C initializer data, lbl_8046E38C, and lbl_80473700.
Matched grBigBlueRoute_8020DA9C and mn_80231634 as null-checked field getters returning 0 for NULL and otherwise returning a struct field at offset x8 or x10.
Matched grHomeRun_8021EAF8 as a stage_info.flags bit set using stage_info.flags |= (1 << 7).
Matched fn_8021A9AC by adding grLast_GroundVars to the Ground union and clearing GET_GROUND(gobj)->gv.last.x0_b0.
Matched a large consecutive family of Mute City functions grMuteCity_801F173C through grMuteCity_801F19F4, each adding one float argument to a different grMc_8049F4B8 field at regular offsets.
Added grmutecity.static.h with a large offset-labeled grMc_8049F4B8_t struct and static storage to support those Mute City field-update matches.
Added itResetVelocity(Item*) inline helper and reused it in existing and newly matched item functions that zero x40_vel.x/y/z.
Matched item velocity-reset routines including it_8029A5E0, it_802C6094, it_802AB128, it_802AB140, it_802B45D0, it_802B37F0, it_802B0580, it_802C4364, and it_802C4980.
Preserved a non-BUGFIX behavior in it_802C4980 where the original code zeros z, y, z rather than x, y, z; the corrected itResetVelocity path is under BUGFIX.
Matched it_802DFFA0 by zeroing the zgshell item variable velocity fields, and expanded itGShell_ItemVars with explicit padding, xE10, and Vec3 vel.
Matched mnStageSel_8025BC08 as an indexed byte getter returning mnStageSel_803F06D0[idx].xB.
Expanded mn_2295.h with many address-commented prototypes and moved existing explanatory declarations to a new mn_2295.dox documentation file.
Updated many headers from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete bool, void, int, short, float, Item_GObj*, Ground_GObj*, or struct-pointer signatures where needed by the matches.
smart_moves: Grouped many tiny stubs with the same code shape into one PR: array loads/stores, field getters, flag writes, float adders, and velocity resets.
Introduced itResetVelocity once and used it to avoid repeating x/y/z zeroing boilerplate, while still preserving the original bug in it_802C4980 outside BUGFIX.
Used local static headers for translation-unit static data and structs, keeping internal data modeling near the code that requires it.
Modeled unknown structs with offset-based fields and padding only as far as the matched routines required, avoiding overnaming unsupported fields.
Added concrete prototypes before or alongside implementations, which likely helped callsite type propagation and prevented mismatches caused by UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
For repeated Mute City 24-byte functions, encoded the regular stride as explicit xOFFSET field writes rather than trying to invent a higher-level array abstraction without evidence.
Used STATIC_ASSERT where a modeled static data structure size was known, notably for lbl_80473700_t.
decomp_lessons: Tiny 24-byte functions are often unlocked by the right data declaration: a single struct field, array type, or static symbol can convert a stub into a trivial C expression.
When matching accessor families, first identify the repeated code shape: NULL guard plus field load, indexed global array load/store, flag OR, or one-field float accumulation.
Do not overinterpret unknown data tables; address-named structs such as lbl_803D5A4C_t with fields x0/x2/x4 are acceptable when semantic names are not evidenced.
For global/static data, a .static.h can be an effective staging area for file-local structs, arrays, initializers, and STATIC_ASSERTs needed for matching.
Header prototype cleanup is part of matching work: replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete signatures can be necessary for codegen and for future callers.
Repeated velocity-zeroing code can be abstracted into a static inline helper if the compiler emits the same stores and callsites already include item data access macros.
Preserve original bugs when matching retail assembly; put the corrected behavior behind BUGFIX rather than silently using the cleaner helper in the normal path.
Bitfield additions to shared unions, such as grLast_GroundVars in Ground.gv, should be minimal and offset-aware to support exact field stores.
Documentation-only movement, such as moving commentary from mn_2295.h into mn_2295.dox, can keep headers focused on declarations while preserving useful notes.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions keep address-based names such as fn_8016F1F0, fn_8021A9AC, and mnStageSel_8025BC08.
Unknown or partially known structs use address/function-derived names such as lbl_803D5A4C_t, lbl_80473700_t, grBigBlueRoute_8020DA9C_t, mn_80231634_t, and grMc_8049F4B8_t.
Unknown struct fields use offset names like x0, x2, x4, x8, x10, and padding fields such as pad_0 and pad_40.
Headers retain address comments on declarations, for example /* 16F1F0 */ and /* 2B45D0 */.
Stage/ground boolean callbacks were clarified from UNK_RET to bool where evidence supported it.
Translation-unit static data declarations were placed in files named with the pattern module.static.h, such as gm_1601.static.h, grmutecity.static.h, and mnstagesel.static.h.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Indexed table getter: return lbl_803D5A4C[idx].x0 with a short return type to match a halfword load.
Indexed global setter: assign lbl_8046E38C[idx] = value for a small store wrapper.
NULL-checked field getter pattern: if arg0 == NULL return 0; otherwise return arg0->x8 or arg0->x10.
Repeated float accumulator pattern: grMc_8049F4B8.xOFFSET += arg0 for many adjacent Mute City routines.
Velocity reset pattern: ip->x40_vel.x = ip->x40_vel.y = ip->x40_vel.z = 0.0F, centralized as itResetVelocity where retail behavior allows.
Retail bug preservation pattern: in it_802C4980, keep z/y/z zeroing under non-BUGFIX and use the clean helper only under BUGFIX.
Union/bitfield access pattern: GET_GROUND(gobj)->gv.last.x0_b0 = false after adding a minimal grLast_GroundVars bitfield struct.
Static data modeling tactic: use explicit initializer tables and STATIC_ASSERTs to align C data layout with expected binary layout.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename grMc_8049F4B8_t fields or convert the Mute City repeated float fields into an array.
Find follow-up work on lbl_803D5A4C_t semantics and gm_1601 static table naming.
Search for later uses or revisions of itResetVelocity in item matching PRs.
Search for PRs that expand grLast_GroundVars beyond x0_b0.
Check whether mn_80231634_t and grBigBlueRoute_8020DA9C_t later receive semantic names.
Look for later cleanup of mn_2295.dox and mn_2295.h prototype organization.
